frameworks.name: computational phenotyping
frameworks.one_line: Mechanistically interpretable model parameters fit to individual behavioral data

frameworks.name: dynamic computational phenotyping
frameworks.one_line: Extends phenotyping to time-varying parameters separating practice and state effects

frameworks.name: psychometric reliability
frameworks.one_line: Frames concern about temporal stability of model-derived parameters

core_question: What sources govern temporal variability of individual computational phenotypes across cognitive tasks?
key_assumptions: Computational model parameters are mechanistically interpretable indices of cognition
Within-person variability decomposes into practice and state-like components
Self-reported mood, habits and activities index latent affective state
Phenotype dimensions can co-vary with internal states over weeks
manipulations: Battery of seven tasks spanning learning, memory, perception, decision making
Weekly reports of mood, habits and daily activities
Decomposition of phenotype variance into practice vs state components
key_findings: Many phenotype dimensions co-varied with practice over weeks
Many phenotype dimensions co-varied with affective valence and arousal
Apparent unreliability of parameters partly reflected previously unmeasured structure
interpretations: Cognitive variability within an individual is fundamentally dynamic rather than noise
Phenotype reliability must be evaluated jointly with practice and affective state
